overview.introduction: Distribute network traffic workloads dynamically using Load Balancers and scale replicas via Auto Scaling.
overview.why_it_matters: Understanding Load Balancers & Auto Scaling allows developers to deploy environments reliably at high speed.
overview.industry_usage: Used by system administrators and platform engineering teams globally.
overview.what_you_will_build: An isolated sandbox folder deploying yaml configurations and targets configs.
overview.prerequisites: Git course and Linux terminal fundamentals.
learning_outcomes: Explain the main concepts of Load Balancers & Auto Scaling.
Structure identity tables, compute servers, networks, and storage systems.
Incorporate monitoring, databases, and multi-tier security firewalls.
Deploy and check state values on target resources.
Adopt clean infrastructure orchestration provisioning workflows.

key_concepts.title: Traffic Elastic Load Balancing
key_concepts.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
why_important: Saves manual server setup configuration time.
key_concepts.explanation: Detailed context regarding Load Balancers & Auto Scaling. Structuring configurations as code blocks improves change transparency.

commands.name: aws
commands.purpose: Execute actions related to Load Balancers & Auto Scaling.
commands.output_explanation: Confirms action step for Load Balancers & Auto Scaling is processed.

key_concepts.pro_tip: Run configuration validations using dry run configurations flags to verify effects before provisioning targets.
key_concepts.best_practices: Version control your manifests and store credentials securely using environment keys.
key_concepts.common_mistakes: Forgetting firewall port rules configuration causing connection timeout failures.
key_concepts.real_world_usage: Cloud architects design multi-tier secure environments using VPCs and virtual instances.
key_concepts.summary: Load Balancers & Auto Scaling configuration forms the building block of secure cloud design.

interactive_examples.objective: Define Load Balancers & Auto Scaling parameters.
interactive_examples.scenario: Review local variables settings in workspace.

steps.explanation: Ensure you are in the correct home directory.

interactive_examples.solution: Verify tasks lists details and paths.

lessons.title: 1. Create an Application Load Balancer
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Create an Application Load Balancer is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Create an ALB named devlab-alb to direct client connections requests.
lessons.hint: Check spelling and spacings of your commands carefully.

lessons.title: 2. Display system diagnostics
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Display system diagnostics is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.hint: Confirm you are in the workspace folder.

lessons.title: 3. List directory contents
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding List directory contents is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Confirm created hosts, templates, or playbooks configurations.
lessons.hint: Run files list to verify existence.

lessons.title: 4. Verify file content displays
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Verify file content displays is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Check the configured playbook.yml file content details.
lessons.example: cat playbook.yml || cat main.tf || cat hosts || echo 'file check'
lessons.hint: Verify the configuration files are correctly saved.
lessons.solution: cat playbook.yml || cat main.tf || cat hosts || echo 'file check'

lessons.title: 5. Display AWS user profile status
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Display AWS user profile status is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Retrieve description properties for IAM user configurations.
lessons.hint: Use active user list query command.

lessons.title: 6. Query active instances status
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Query active instances status is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Check list outcomes of configured virtual machine targets.
lessons.hint: Ensure instance describes options are run.

lessons.title: 7. Query S3 object storage state
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Query S3 object storage state is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Check bucket metadata and active storage lists.
lessons.hint: Run storage check using aws s3 ls command.

lessons.title: 8. Mini Challenge: Create an Auto Scaling Group configuration
lessons.definition: Balancing incoming HTTP/TCP connection requests across virtual machine pools.
lessons.explanation: Understanding Mini Challenge: Create an Auto Scaling Group configuration is critical for configuring this step.
lessons.instruction: Create an ASG template specifying min/max/desired bounds for computing capacity.
lessons.hint: Complete the mini challenge criteria to validate lesson progress.
